channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Strands #770 Hints and Answers for Today, April 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #770 for Sunday, April 12, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 12, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #770 are all related to tools needed to get ready for your day.

General Strands Hints For April 12

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Two of the answers today center around tools used to groom hair.
  • Hint 2: One of the answers is in the sentence for the previous hint.
  • Hint 3: The Spanagram is the name of a 2022 single by The Interrupters.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #770 for April 12, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 12, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • THEME
  • ROOM
  • TIME
  • RUSH
  • BEAUTY

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #770 On April 12, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • PREEN
  • STYLE
  • GROOM
  • BEAUTIFY
  • PRIMP
  • BRUSH
  • COMB
  • SPANAGRAM: INTHEMIRROR

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #770?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on April 13 will be Strands Game #771.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Xbox Showcase Reportedly Coming Next Week, Metro 2039 May Be Revealed
item.content:encoded:
NateTheHate provides an update on when players can expect the next Xbox Games Showcase. Reportedly, it will serve as the host for the reveal of Metro 2039.

In response to Jez Corden’s claim that there will be a small Xbox show next week, Nate took to Twitter to say the show will be held on Thursday, April 16. He also said the show would serve as the venue to reveal Metro 2039. 2039 is the rumored name for the fourth mainline Metro game release.

As Nate has also claimed that there could be a Sony PlayStation State of Play presentation on Thursday, April 16, he once again stressed that he has not been able to confirm the date of that show. He’s also unable to confirm the specific branding of the Xbox showcase that may take place.

More on the Upcoming Metro Game

A recent data leak of 120 GB claims to have uncovered a canned version of the fourth Metro game, which is believed to have been shelved by 4A Games in 2022. Some features of the allegedly leaked game include following the storyline of the Metro 2034 book and fully reviving Hunter.

Additionally, the game would offer an open world that spans both the Metro tunnels and the overworld. For more information about this leak, click here. Reports that the game could be revealed next week first surfaced on Twitter from @AlexandreNGamR. As of yet, Insider Gaming has not been able to confirm this news.

NateTheHate’s Nintendo Claims for 2026

NateTheHate has been busy lately. He recently released a podcast in which he shared all the news he’s heard about the major Nintendo releases for 2026. Of note, those releases include the return of Star Fox in a game that will reportedly be revealed this month and launch in summer 2026. Also, Nate claims that the next 3D Mario title won’t be released until 2027.

Whatever happens with the future Microsoft showcase or Sony State of Play, Insider Gaming will bring live coverage of either show. For more, learn about the rumored upcoming Star Fox title.

Are you excited for the potential upcoming Xbox showcase? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Former Xbox Exec Says Developers Didn’t Want a Sony Monopoly
item.content:encoded:
Ed Fries led the team at Microsoft that launched the first Xbox in 2001. Now, a quarter century later, he’s opening up about the early days of Xbox. Specifically, he discusses how some companies sought to prevent monopolies in the 2000s but had to be cautious about the level of support they gave the new challenger.

Xbox is a staple of the gaming industry today, but in 2001, it was anything but. With Sega having recently exited the console race and less than a decade separating the launch of the Xbox from the failure of platforms like the 3DO and the Philips CDi, it wasn’t clear that computer giant Microsoft would succeed in the video game space.

Ed Fries Recalls Visits with Japanese Companies

During an appearance on The Expansion Pass, Ed Fries noted that the Final Fantasy franchise was a missed opportunity for Xbox and that he tried to get the popular JRPG series to become exclusive to Microsoft Hardware. However, Square Enix would have been leaving too much money on the table.

Furthermore, Fries discussed conversations he had with Japanese game development companies, including Square Enix, Capcom, Konami, and Tecmo. When talking about Square, he noted that certain conversations were always difficult because Square wanted Sony to face competition, but they weren’t allowed to be too overt in their support for the new Microsoft console.

“I would go out to Japan fairly regularly and meet with Konami, meet with Capcom, meet with Square, meet with Sega, and some of them, we were able to do deals with some of them, but we weren’t with others. They were able to do some deals, after I left, with Square, but it was always a tough discussion because they wanted Sony to have competition and wouldn’t be too overt in their support for Xbox. They couldn’t make it too obvious that they were supporting Xbox.

Additionally, he noted that Tecmo did Dead or Alive games on Xbox to send a subtle message to Sony.

“They did it, kind of, to tweak Sony because they wanted Sony to have competitors because otherwise they’re a monopoly and monopolies, you know, just do whatever they want.”

Today, Xbox-published titles are on Sony hardware. Even though Xbox is still a console manufacturer, it has found great success porting its titles to PlayStation. Asha Sharma, the current Microsoft gaming CEO, believes the Xbox brand can return to prominence by focusing on hardcore Xbox gamers. Learn more here.

How do you feel about this peek behind the curtain into the early days of Xbox? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1759: Hints and Answer for April 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1759 for April 13, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, April 13, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1759 is a challenging one. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 5.3 out of 6 guesses. That makes it over a full guess harder than the 4-guess average from April 12.

General Wordle Hints For April 13

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It describes something small, delicate, or slightly magical in appearance.
  • Hint 2: Often used to describe facial features or a certain kind of charm.
  • Hint 3: The word has roots in folklore and fantasy.
  • Hint 4: You might associate it with tiny, playful mythical beings.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1759 for April 13, 2026.

Starter Words For April 13, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • LINED
  • FINAL
  • FLIET
  • INLET
  • FLING

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1759 On April 13, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: ELFIN

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

ELFIN is an adjective that can mean “of or like an elf” or “small and charmingly spritely, merry, or mischievous.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1758 on April 12, 2026

Answer: ALLEY

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1759?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on April 14 will be Wordle Game #1760.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.title: Upcoming Fable Game Delayed Internally, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
The upcoming Fable game from Xbox has reportedly been delayed internally. However, that does not necessarily mean that the game won’t release in 2026.

The Fable franchise was once a staple of Microsoft’s early Xbox days. 25 years later, a Fable reboot is on the way, with a 2026 release. However, a new claim suggests that the game may have been pushed back internally. Largely, this is because the team behind the upcoming release is concerned about the launch of Grand Theft Auto 6, due out in November of this year.

Fable’s Reported Internal Delay at Xbox

Jeff Grubb recently noted that he’s heard claims of the game being delayed internally. However, according to him, Microsoft still has plans to release Fable in 2026. The game could potentially target a launch in the final month of the year.

“Apparently, they’re still trying to get it out this year, but they are worried about the launch of GTA 6. And so if it’s getting delayed beyond the release of GTA 6, that could push it into December, which might make it a prime candidate to get delayed into 2027,” said Grubb.

The game was recently showcased during the January Xbox Developer Direct. That is when Playground Games announced that the game was planned for 2026. At the time, it was noted that the title would be released on PC and Xbox Series Consoles in Autumn 2026. They could technically release it in the first three weeks of December and still claim that the release window is accurate.

Long Road to Fable’s Release

The game was originally announced in 2020. However, players had to wait until 2023 to see a trailer debut. It was then slated for release in 2025, but was deleted early in that year and rescheduled for 2026. Then-Xbox Studios boss Craig Duncan claimed the title would be worth the wait.

Asha Sharma recently took the reins at Xbox following the retirement of Phil Spencer and Sarah Bond’s subsequent resignation. She believes the brand’s return to prominence starts with video games. Read her comments here.

For more on Fable, check out news concerning the morality dynamics in the upcoming game. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the latest in the story and report any and all updates.

Are you excited for Fable? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ‘Tarkov in Space’ Revealed as Fragmentary Order, a Sci-Fi ‘Combat Simulator’
item.content:encoded:
Rant Gaming, in association with Cor3, has unveiled Fragmentary Order, long dubbed ‘Escape from Tarkov in space.’ Over the last few months, Nikita Buyanov, the head of the studio at Battlestate Games, has been teasing a link to Cor3, a mysterious entity tied to a cryptic ARG, a puzzling universe, and a sci-fi theme that many thought would be his next game.

Today’s announcement, which went hand-in-hand with a seven-minute CGI trailer, uncovered Fragmentary Order, a realistic, hardcore, sci-fi multiplayer FPS ‘exploring tactical simulation, survival, and risk.’

But from what we know, it seems like a more complicated way to describe an extraction shooter in space.

What is Fragmentary Order?

Rant Gaming has officially revealed Fragmentary Order after months of puzzles, teasers, and world-building. In a new press release, the game’s gist was detailed:

Set in the year 2251, Fragmentary Order takes place in a future shaped by the rise of Core – a powerful organization that once saved humanity from collapse and unified a fractured world under a single technological and economic system.

Over the following centuries, Core led humanity into a new era: colonization of the Solar System, advanced biotech, and the creation of CORIE – a global framework where everything, including warfare, is structured, monetized, and controlled.

But control is no longer absolute.

As influence fractures and new powers emerge, conflict returns – not as chaos, but as industry.

Players take the role of a ‘Replicated Entity’, deploying clone assets remotely into battlefields spread around the solar system. They’ll control their constructs from the safety of a bunker, carrying out contracts, scavenging resources, and (hopefully) surviving fights.

The gameplay loop is reminiscent of Escape from Tarkov:

  • Infiltrate contested zones
  • Complete objectives, secure resources
  • Utilize operational ground assets
  • Find a way to complete your mission and get out alive

It was said that Fragmentary Order will be designed for players seeking depth and realism. It utilizes advanced weapon behavior, complex health systems, large-scale maps, vehicles and expanded mobility, excessive weapon customization, and the use of cutting-edge technology.

The game’s neo-economic structure means that users will need to build relationships with corporations, lean on advanced technologies, and expand their personal infrastructure.

It’s all still reading very much like Escape from Tarkov in space at this point.

Fragmentary Order is rooted in the Cold War, after which the world was plunged into a global collapse, brought back from the brink by a mega-corporation that influenced rapid technological expansion and fuelled a space colonization effort.

The team at Rant Gaming revealed that the ARG found on the COR3 website is still of the utmost importance. It’s how users will get access to the earliest iterations of the game, proving their ‘readiness and right to be in the first row.’

To get access to Fragmentary Order, you’ll need to complete the following steps:

  • Study the system: Dive into the hidden layers and extract classified lore fragments.
  • Set the groundwork: Hoard resources, establish credit flows, and lock in exclusive assets before the masses flood in.
  • Elevate your standing: Grind reputation and earn ranks. Your actions right now will shape the meta and forge your legacy.

Do you think Fragmentary Order sounds like your kind of game? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Road to Vostok’s entire roadmap has been funded

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP
